pattern: Sort, then greedily extend the selected suffix from right to left — keep adding the next dish as long as the running suffix sum stays positive.
hints: The optimal selection is always a suffix of the sorted array — discard only the lowest-satisfaction dishes from the front.
After sorting, compute the suffix sum from right to left; each time you include one more dish, the total like-time coefficient increases by the current suffix sum.
Keep a running suffix_sum and result; iterate from largest to smallest, adding each satisfaction to suffix_sum, then to result only while suffix_sum > 0.

edge_inputs.description: all negative — best is to cook no dishes
all positive — cook all, sorted ascending
single negative dish — answer is zero
mix of positives and negatives — dropping the worst negative improves the result
one large negative offset by many small positives
two dishes summing to exactly zero — selecting one is better than none
adding one more dish keeps suffix_sum at zero — should not be included

stress.description: large random satisfaction array with mixed positive and negative values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxSatisfaction(self, satisfaction):
        # Brute force: try all 2^n subsets but that is too slow for n=500.
        # Instead use DP: for each possible count k of dishes selected (the k largest),
        # compute the like-time coefficient and return the maximum.
        satisfaction.sort()
        n = len(satisfaction)
        best = 0
        for k in range(1, n + 1):
            # Select the k largest (rightmost) dishes in sorted order
            # Assign times 1..k to them in sorted order
            total = 0
            for i, dish in enumerate(satisfaction[n - k:], 1):
                total += i * dish
            if total > best:
                best = total
        return best
